'''Pax Bonkik''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]], and along with his brother, [[Trax Bonkik]], served as a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] to the [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Mars Guo/Legends|Mars Guo]]. Guo worked the siblings to near-exhaustion. Pax eventually struck up a friendship with the racer [[Teemto Pagalies/Legends|Teemto Pagalies]], who abhorred slavery, especially since the object of his affections, [[Ann Gella/Legends|Ann Gella]], was herself a slave. During the [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] of [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Guo sustained crippling damage from another Podracer, [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]]. While Guo was healing from his wounds, Sebulba arranged to have Guo's possessions auctioned off.

Pagalies, who had himself survived a crash during the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|race]], was only able to free Pax, with Trax being sold off to Sebulba. Pax, however, was determined to save his brother, and worked for Pagalies until he had earned enough to repay the racer. When Pagalies won the [[Aleen Classic]], he had enough credits to free Ann Gella, but at the last moment, he chose to free Trax, finally reuniting him with his brother.

==Biography==
===Early life and service under Guo===
The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Pax Bonkik hailed from [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]], and had a bond with his brother, [[Trax Bonkik|Trax]]. Their parents were [[Spice/Legends|spice]] addicts and shiftless, selling their children into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]. Pax decided to look out for his brother during those times, and the two drifted from owner to owner, eventually learning skills to increase their worth. They primarily worked as [[Technician/Legends|mechanics]], and ended up working for a minor league [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]], [[Mars Guo/Legends|Mars Guo]].<ref name="db" />

Guo forced the siblings to work to near-exhaustion on his [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]], a [[Plug-2 Behemoth|Plug-2 ''Behemoth'']], and the pair were also subjected to Guo's constant boasting. In a rare moment of peace and quiet, Pax befriended [[Teemto Pagalies/Legends|Teemto Pagalies]], another Podracer. Pagalies was staunchly against slavery, particularly because the object of his affections, [[Ann Gella/Legends|Ann Gella]], was in fact a slave of another Podracer, [[Sebulba/Legends|Sebulba]].<ref name="db" />

===Boonta Eve Classic===
[[File:Pax_and_Trax_Bonkik_TPM.png|thumb|300px|Pax and Trax Bonkik worked as mechanics for Mars Guo prior to and during 32 BBY's Boonta Eve Classic.]]
In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Guo intended to compete in the [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], one of the largest and most prestigious of all [[Podracing/Legends|Podraces]].<ref name="rr">''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]''</ref> Shortly before the race, Trax entered into an argument with Guo, and the latter mercilessly beat the Rodian mercilessly and claimed that after the race, he would sell Trax off, thereby separating the siblings.<ref name="db" />

Pax, seeking to avert such an occurrence, contacted Pagalies, who was also participating in the race. He explained the situation, and also told Pagalies that Guo had his eyes on Gella, and likely intended to purchase her from Sebulba. Pagalies was skeptical, but he disliked the idea of having the Bonkiks separated, or worse, the thought of Gella in the clutches of Guo.<ref name="db" />

During the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|race]], Guo's Podracer was destroyed by Sebulba.<ref name="The Phantom Menace">{{Film|I}}</ref> While this could have proved advantageous for the Bonkiks, it turned out to be disastrous. A bitter Sebulba,<ref name="db" /> who had lost the race to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], a local boy,<ref name="The Phantom Menace" /> arranged to have Guo's possessions auctioned while Guo lay in the infirmary.<ref name="db" /> Pagalies had also sustained the loss of his Podracer during the tumultuous race,<ref name="The Phantom Menace" /> and was only able to afford to buy Pax at the auction, while Trax fell into the possession of Sebulba.<ref name="db" />

===Service under Pagalies===
Despite the fact that he was free, Pax decided to stay on with Pagalies, and served as his mechanic, traveling the racing circuit and on the lookout for Sebulba or Trax. Eventually, Pax was able to raise enough credits to pay Pagalies back for his kindness on Tatooine. Pagalies won the [[Aleen Classic]], and was finally able to purchase Ann Gella's freedom. At the last minute, however, Pagalies relented, and purchased Trax, reuniting the Rodians. The siblings would never again be apart.<ref name="db" />

==Personality and traits==
Pax Bonkik had a strong companionship with his brother, Trax. They stayed together through their slavery, and the well-being of Trax was high in Pax's priorities. He became friends with Teemto Pagalies, and sought out the latter's help when he felt that Guo would sell off Trax and purchase Ann Gella. When the machinations of Sebulba threatened to tear them apart, and Pagalies was only able to pay for freeing Pax, the Rodian stayed in the Podracer's company, and even though he did not have to, worked as a mechanic for Pagalies. Pax Bonkik stood at a height of 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] and had [[Color/Legends|green]] skin and black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="db" />

==Equipment==
Pax Bonkik was armed with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], and used repair tools during his work as a mechanic.<ref name="Hasbro">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: 30th Anniversary Collection|pack=#54: Pax Bonkik (Rodian Podracer Mechanic)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TAC54paxbonkik.asp}}</ref> He also wore a green [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] with a blue vest.<ref name="db" />

==Behind the scenes==
Pax Bonkik first appeared in the [[1999]] [[prequel trilogy]] film {{Film|I}}, during the Boonta Eve Podrace scene.<ref name="The Phantom Menace" /> On [[August 30]], [[2005]],<ref name="winners">{{SWArchive|url=databank/updates/news20050930.html|text=Hyperspace Presents: What's The Story, Round 2}}</ref> the character was first named, when user [[javajawas]], through the [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature "[[What's The Story?]]," gave an entry for Bonkik.<ref name="db" />
